signals.title: Microsoft and OpenAI end their exclusive revenue-sharing deal — "Next phase of Microsoft partnership" announced
signals.description: Bloomberg (2026-04-27) reports Microsoft will stop sharing revenue with OpenAI, ending the exclusivity structure that defined the foundational Microsoft-OpenAI relationship since 2019. OpenAI published a companion post titled "Next phase of Microsoft partnership." The mechanism: the original deal gave Microsoft exclusive API resale rights and a revenue share in exchange for compute commitments (now dwarfed by OpenAI's Cerebras $20B+ chip purchase and reported $50B NVIDIA round). The new structure removes exclusivity on API resale — meaning other cloud providers can now offer OpenAI APIs competitively — and ends the direct revenue-sharing arrangement. → This is the formal dissolution of the "one hyperscaler / exclusive cloud" model that powered OpenAI's scale-up from 2019-2025. Combined with Anthropic's dual-hyperscaler (Amazon + Google) non-exclusive structure, the frontier-lab capital model has now fully shifted to multi-cloud. Microsoft retains its equity stake and remains a major compute partner, but the exclusive commercial advantage is gone. Watch: how OpenAI prices API access on Azure vs. other clouds within 30 days, and whether Azure loses OpenAI model margin to Google Cloud / AWS now that exclusivity is lifted.   [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-27/microsoft-to-stop-sharing-revenue-with-main-ai-partner-openai) · [OpenAI](https://openai.com/index/next-phase-of-microsoft-partnership/)
signals.actionLine: This is the formal dissolution of the "one hyperscaler / exclusive cloud" model that powered OpenAI's scale-up from 2019-2025.

signals.title: China's NDRC blocks Meta's $2B acquisition of Manus AI — formal security review prohibits foreign acquisition
signals.description: China's National Development and Reform Commission issued a formal security review decision on 2026-04-27 prohibiting Meta's acquisition of the Manus AI project. The decision language: "The Office of the Working Mechanism for Security Review of Foreign Investment has, in accordance with laws and regulations, issued a decision prohibiting the foreign-investor acquisition of the Manus project, and requires the relevant parties to cancel the acquisition transaction." Bloomberg confirmed the $2B price. Manus is the Chinese AI agent company (browser-use, multi-step task automation) that gained viral traction in Q1 2026 with its "general AI agent" claim. Commenters note the block is "about data" not product quality. → China has now exercised formal NDRC security review to block an AI agent acquisition — the first time this mechanism has been deployed against a US buyer for an AI agent company specifically. The rationale is almost certainly the training data and behavioral-trace data Manus has collected from users, which the NDRC treats as a strategic national asset. This establishes a new precedent: AI agent companies with large Chinese user bases are non-acquirable by US-domiciled entities regardless of price. Watch for: whether this triggers a broader Chinese regulatory sweep of AI agent data-export provisions.   [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-27/china-blocks-meta-s-2-billion-acquisition-of-ai-startup-manus) · [NDRC decision](https://x.com/catmangox/status/2048680484037935200)
signals.actionLine: China has now exercised formal NDRC security review to block an AI agent acquisition — the first time this mechanism has been deployed against a US buyer for an AI agent company specifically.

signals.title: Dirac open-source agent scores 65.2% on TerminalBench — beats Google's official 47.8% and closed-source Junie CLI's 64.3%
signals.description: GodelNumbering published results on 2026-04-27 from a fully leaderboard-compliant run of their open-source Dirac agent on the TerminalBench coding-agent benchmark (TerminalBench evaluates agents on real terminal tasks requiring file manipulation, environment setup, debugging, and multi-step execution). Key claim: "Absolutely no `{agents/skills}.md` files were inserted at any point. No cheating mechanisms whatsoever." GitHub codebase is fully public; the run matches the GitHub version exactly. The submitted PR on the HuggingFace leaderboard has sat unprocessed for 8 days, so results are self-reported. The author's key observation: "It is astounding how much the harness matters — the agent framework (how you structure tool calling, error recovery, retry logic) dominates raw model quality on these tasks." → The benchmark result itself needs leaderboard verification, but the technical finding — that harness engineering dominates model quality for terminal-task agents — is significant regardless of exact score. If confirmed, Dirac becomes the first open-source agent to beat closed commercial agents on a real terminal-task benchmark. Demonstrates that the open-source coding-agent gap is now about harness engineering, not model access.   [GitHub](https://github.com/dirac-run/dirac)
signals.actionLine: The benchmark result itself needs leaderboard verification, but the technical finding — that harness engineering dominates model quality for terminal-task agents — is significant regardless of exact score.

signals.title: Claude 4.7 identifies writer Kelsey Piper from 125 words of unpublished text — stylometric fingerprinting at frontier scale
signals.description: Writer Kelsey Piper (Vox Future Perfect) pasted 125 words of an unpublished political column into Claude 4.7 via the API (logged-out, friend's laptop, incognito, off-genre). Claude named her. ChatGPT and Gemini guessed wrong on the same test. The test design methodically eliminated alternative explanations: account-based identification (logged out), browser fingerprinting (raw API), IP identification (friend's laptop), topical recognition (genre-swapped prose). The only remaining identification channel is the prose structure itself. The author's inference: "A model that's encoded prose pattern structure deeply enough to identify a writer is also, plausibly, more rigid when generating" — implying the same feature that enables stylometric recognition may also reduce output diversity. → Stylometry (the identification of individuals from writing patterns — rhythm, word choice, sentence structure) has existed as a forensic technique for decades, but required specialized tools and large corpora. Claude 4.7 appears to operate as a zero-shot stylometric classifier at 125 words. If reproducible, this means frontier models have absorbed enough of the public literary record to recognize writers from minimal unpublished samples. The privacy implication: users cannot interact anonymously with Claude if they have a traceable published writing history.   [The Argument Magazine](https://www.theargumentmag.com/p/i-can-never-talk-to-an-ai-anonymously)
signals.actionLine: Stylometry (the identification of individuals from writing patterns — rhythm, word choice, sentence structure) has existed as a forensic technique for decades, but required specialized tools and large corpora.

signals.title: YourMemory: AI memory with biological decay — 52% Recall@5 on LoCoMo, 84% token reduction vs static RAG
signals.description: Show HN from SachitRafa (2026-04-26): implements the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve (the empirically measured pattern of how human memory decays exponentially with time unless reinforced — a memory's "strength" decreases as a function of time since last retrieval) to manage an AI agent's memory context. Each memory is assigned a strength score that decays over time; each retrieval reinforces and flattens the decay curve (spaced repetition). Unused data eventually crosses a pruning threshold. Adds a graph layer over the vector store to solve the "logical neighbor" problem where semantic search misses nodes that are contextually related but not semantically similar. Results: 52% Recall@5 on LoCoMo benchmark (vs ~30% for stateless vector stores), 84% reduction in token waste. Built as a local-first MCP server using DuckDB. The key framing: "what to forget is just as critical as what to remember" for long-running agent projects. → Demonstrates that biological memory models are practically applicable to agent systems, not just theoretically motivated. The MCP-server implementation means this drops into any MCP-compatible agent stack without modification. Compare to Stash (Apache 2.0, captured April 26) and Mem0 — the open-source memory layer for agents is now a multi-project ecosystem with meaningfully different approaches.   [GitHub](https://github.com/sachitrafa/YourMemory)
signals.actionLine: Demonstrates that biological memory models are practically applicable to agent systems, not just theoretically motivated.

signals.title: HauhauCS publishes plagiarized abliteration package — AGPL-3.0 violation of Heretic confirmed by original author
signals.description: HauhauCS (a HuggingFace account with 5M+ combined monthly downloads across 22 uncensored models) published `reaper-abliteration` on HuggingFace and PyPI. Philipp Emanuel Weidmann, author of the AGPL-3.0-licensed Heretic abliteration tool, analyzed the recovered source code and confirmed: 7/7 module filenames preserved, 30/32 refusal markers character-for-character identical, and the entire analyser geometry pipeline (geometric median computation, PaCMAP parameters) reproduced step-for-step — including a specific combination "never seen in abliteration literature outside Heretic." Weidmann's conclusion: "I can say with certainty that this package was plagiarized from Heretic, and then probably refactored using an LLM in an attempt to hide this." → Highlights an emerging risk in the open-source ML ecosystem: LLM-assisted refactoring is being used to launder AGPL-licensed code into nominally new packages that evade attribution detection. The 5M+ downloads on the plagiarizer's account gives this commercial scale. This is the second notable AI-OSS IP incident in the scan window (after Bitwarden CLI MCP supply-chain compromise April 23).   [Analysis](https://dreamfast.github.io/reaper-analysis)
signals.actionLine: Highlights an emerging risk in the open-source ML ecosystem: LLM-assisted refactoring is being used to launder AGPL-licensed code into nominally new packages that evade attribution detection.

signals.title: Luce DFlash: 2× speculative decoding speedup for Qwen3.6-27B on a single RTX 3090
signals.description: sandropuppo released Luce DFlash (MIT license, C++/CUDA, standalone, no Python runtime): a speculative decoding implementation (speculative decoding — a small "draft" model proposes candidate tokens, a larger "verify" model accepts or rejects them in parallel, achieving faster generation than autoregressive one-by-one token prediction) for Qwen3.6-27B on a single 24GB RTX 3090. Benchmarks: 2.24× speedup on HumanEval, 1.99× on Math500, 1.71× on GSM8K — mean 1.98× across benchmarks. The system compresses the KV cache (the per-token attention memory that grows with context length) to TQ3_0 (3.5 bits per value) to fit 256K context in 24GB VRAM and uses sliding-window flash attention at decode. Results are bit-identical to autoregressive output in AR verification mode. Constraints: CUDA-only, greedy verify only, requires gated z-lab draft model. → Demonstrates that 2× inference speedup is achievable on consumer GPUs without cloud infrastructure, no retraining, MIT license. The C++/CUDA approach (no Python, no vLLM, no llama.cpp dependency) makes this embeddable in native applications. Combined with the multi-GPU VRAM pooling technique below, the local inference frontier is moving significantly this week.   [Reddit](https://reddit.com/r/LocalLLaMA/comments/1sx8uok/luce_dflash_qwen3627b_at_up_to_2x_throughput_on_a)
signals.actionLine: Demonstrates that 2× inference speedup is achievable on consumer GPUs without cloud infrastructure, no retraining, MIT license.

signals.title: DeepSeek V4 ships — 1.6T-parameter open-weight MoE with functionally agentic 1M-token context, FP4-Blackwell-native, MIT license, day-0 SGLang + Miles RL framework
signals.description: V4-Pro = 1.6T total / 49B active (Mixture of Experts — only 49B of the 1.6T parameters fire per token, keeping inference cost linked to ~49B compute while capacity is 1.6T). V4-Flash = 284B total / 13B active. Trained on 32T+ tokens with the Muon optimizer. Three architecture innovations stacked: (1) **DSA — DeepSeek Sparse Attention** with token-wise compression, (2) **hybrid attention** alternating Compressed Sparse Attention (4× compression with FP4 lightning indexer) and Heavily Compressed Attention (128× compression), (3) **Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections** (mHC, generalized residuals). Net result: V4-Pro uses **27% of V3.2's per-token inference FLOPs** and **10% of V3.2's KV cache memory** (KV cache = the per-token memory state that grows linearly with context — DeepSeek's compression makes 1M context economically viable on commodity hardware where it previously was not). V4-Pro decodes at 199 tok/sec on B200 (drops <10% to 180 at 900K context); V4-Flash hits 266 tok/sec on H200. **FP4 expert weights designed for Blackwell** — model is co-designed with US-export-restricted chip silicon; production-deployed on **Huawei Ascend 950** with full hardware-specific tuning ("DeepSeek spent months reworking software stack for Ascend"). Performance: "Beats all current open models in Math/STEM/Coding, rivaling top closed-source models." Knowledge trails only Gemini-3.1-Pro. Native integration with Claude Code, OpenClaw, OpenCode. Pricing: V4-Pro $3.48/M output tokens (vs OpenAI $30, Anthropic $25, Moonshot Kimi $4); V4-Flash $0.14/$0.28 per Mtok (Haiku-tier). MIT license. V3/V3.2 hard-retire 2026-07-24, forcing customer migration in 90 days. **Miles RL framework** open-sourced same day: full DP/TP/SP/EP/PP/CP parallelism, FP8 rollout with BF16/FP8 training, R3 (Rollout Routing Replay), step-0 train-inference drift ~0.02-0.03. → First open-weight model where 1M context is *functionally agentic*. The "FP4 weights for Blackwell" + "production on Ascend 950" combo is a direct shot at NVIDIA's monetization layer — frontier-class open weights now run on the chip the US bans for export. Same-week trifecta with GPT-5.5 and Google-Anthropic $40B means the Chinese open-source frontier closed the gap to weeks, not quarters.     [Source](https://api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news260424) · [HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro) · [SGLang/Miles](https://www.lmsys.org/blog/2026-04-25-deepseek-v4/)
signals.actionLine: First open-weight model where 1M context is *functionally agentic*.

signals.title: OpenAI GPT-5.5 + GPT-5.5 Pro launched — first fully retrained base since GPT-4.5; MCP-native; computer use, hosted shell, apply patch, Skills built into the API
signals.description: Released 2026-04-23 to paid tiers (Plus/Pro/Business/Enterprise) and to API. Natively omnimodal (text/image/audio/video unified). 1M-token context, image input, structured outputs, function calling, prompt caching, batch, tool search, **built-in computer use, hosted shell, apply patch, Skills, MCP, web search**. Reasoning effort defaults to `medium` (same change Anthropic's postmortem just attributed to a regression). GPT-5.5 Pro is a higher-compute reasoning tier via the Responses API. Pricing $5/$30 per Mtok input/output (GPT-5.4 was $2.50/$10) — frontier price went **up**, not down — but OpenAI claims token efficiency offsets it. Performance: Terminal-Bench 2.0 **82.7%** (vs Opus 4.7 69.4%, Gemini 3.1 Pro 68.5%); Expert-SWE 73.1%; OSWorld-Verified 78.7%; SWE-Bench Pro 58.6% (Opus 4.7 = 64.3%); FrontierMath Tier 1-3 = 51.7%. NVIDIA confirms GPT-5.5 deployed to production on **GB200 NVL72** racks (35× lower cost/Mtok and 50× higher tokens/sec/MW vs prior gen); 10,000+ NVIDIA employees got early access, debugging cycles "days to hours." Codex now powered by GPT-5.5 with NVIDIA marketplace + persistent memory + MCP support + realtime API + sandbox/app-server controls; Codex WAU at 4M+ ("up from 3M, +1M/month"). → "Built-in computer use + hosted shell + apply patch + Skills" means agentic infrastructure is now baked into the OpenAI API — no third-party agent framework required. OpenAI fully embracing Anthropic's MCP protocol confirms MCP as the cross-vendor standard. The price increase is the regime change worth tracking — efficiency improvements no longer flow to customers automatically; OpenAI has the leverage to charge more for compute-heavy frontier.    [Source](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-5/) · [NVIDIA](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/openai-codex-gpt-5-5-ai-agents/) · [Codex update](https://openai.com/index/codex-for-almost-everything/)

signals.title: Anthropic publishes 7-week Claude Code regression postmortem — three compounding silent-default bugs across reasoning_effort, prompt-caching, and verbosity
signals.description: Postmortem published 2026-04-23 covering the March 4 → April 20 quality-regression complaints. (1) `reasoning_effort` default flipped from `high` to `medium` between March 4 and April 7 — degraded coding quality silently; (2) prompt-caching bug between March 26 and April 10 — caching mis-fired every turn, dropping prior reasoning state each step (made Claude appear "forgetful"); (3) verbosity prompt between April 16 and April 20 capped inter-tool-call output at "≤25 words" — reduced intelligence by ~3% across all model versions. All resolved by 2026-04-20 in v2.1.116. Anthropic reset usage limits for all subscribers as compensation. → Validates 7 weeks of accelerating r/ClaudeAI / HN cancellation reports — the cause was three independent silent-default changes shipping without rollout testing. The reasoning_effort default is exactly the change OpenAI's GPT-5.5 just made (defaults to `medium`) — OpenAI presumably more confident in its eval suite, but this is now the first failure mode every frontier lab will be watching. The postmortem also lands the same week as a separate Tell HN report that Claude 4.7 ignores stop hooks, suggesting the eval gap may not be fully closed. Trust hit during the worst possible week (GPT-5.5 + DeepSeek V4 launch).   [Source](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/april-23-postmortem)
signals.actionLine: April 20 quality-regression complaints.

signals.title: TSMC declines ASML's High-NA EUV machines through 2029, citing cost; A13 node pushed to 2029
signals.description: Announced 2026-04-22. ASML's High-NA EUV (Extreme Ultraviolet, the lithography tech for sub-3nm chips — "high numerical aperture" lets it print finer features than current low-NA EUV) is the flagship machine of the global semi-equipment industry, priced at ~€350M each. TSMC will stay on existing low-NA EUV through at least the 2026-2028 ramp. The leading-edge **A13 node now slated for 2029 production**. → ASML's flagship machine is effectively shelved by its largest customer. Confirms that frontier-chip cost is now binding even at TSMC scale. Pair with Samsung's 2nm yield benchmarked at ~55% (TSMC ~65-70%, Intel 18A ~55-60%) — Samsung Foundry remains non-viable for high-volume 2nm despite political pressure to diversify away from TSMC. The MTIA (Meta), Maia (Microsoft), Trainium (AWS), AI5 (Tesla) custom-chip plans all assume TSMC capacity will keep up; the High-NA postponement is the first quantitative signal that the lithography roadmap can't outrun demand.  [Source](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-22/tsmc-says-asml-s-latest-chipmaking-gear-is-too-pricey-to-use)

signals.description: Released 2026-04-24. `/multitask` deploys **async subagents** to parallelize requests *within* the agent window — productizes the parallel-agent-orchestration pattern. **Worktrees** execute isolated background tasks across branches with one-click foreground promotion. **Multi-root workspaces** let one agent session operate across multiple folders/repos without retargeting (cross-repo frontend↔backend↔shared-libs changes). → Cursor moves from "single-agent-per-repo" to "agent-fleet-across-repos" — closes a structural gap with Claude Code/Codex on polyrepo enterprise codebases. Pairs with Codex CLI shipping `codex_hooks` stable + first-class Amazon Bedrock SigV4 auth (April 24-25), and AWS Bedrock AgentCore GA's Managed Agent Harness (April 22) — the agentic-coding stack is now productized end-to-end across the three major IDEs and AWS's enterprise-distribution channel.   [Source](https://cursor.com/changelog/04-24-26)
signals.actionLine: Cursor moves from "single-agent-per-repo" to "agent-fleet-across-repos" — closes a structural gap with Claude Code/Codex on polyrepo enterprise codebases.

Same week, AWS shipped: SageMaker AI inference recommendations with EAGLE 3.0 + AIPerf (2× throughput at equal latency on H100, halving inference cost), Visier-Quick HR-tech MCP integration (April 24), Bedrock + Mem0 organizational memory pattern at TrendMicro (production deployment). signals.title: DeepMind Decoupled DiLoCo paper — asynchronous distributed pre-training across heterogeneous accelerators with strictly zero global downtime
signals.description: arXiv 2604.21428 submitted 2026-04-23. Authors: Arthur Douillard, **Jeff Dean**, Marc'Aurelio Ranzato, Arthur Szlam et al. Combines Pathways (asynchronous orchestration across heterogeneous accelerators) with DiLoCo (bandwidth-minimizing distributed training). Minimum quorum, adaptive grace window, dynamic token-weighted merging. Achieves **strictly zero global downtime across millions of simulated chips**, matching synchronous baselines on dense and MoE architectures. → Direct enabler of planet-scale federated pre-training (multi-region runs without the SPMD synchronization barrier that bottlenecks current TPU/GPU clusters). The "decentralized training" frontier edge is now demonstrably operational at frontier-lab scale. Implication: Anthropic's 8.5 GW Google TPU + 5 GW Amazon Trainium combined fleet can run as a single training job rather than two separate ones — straight upgrade to the compute-for-equity structure economics.     [Source](https://deepmind.google/blog/decoupled-diloco/) · [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.21428)
signals.actionLine: Direct enabler of planet-scale federated pre-training (multi-region runs without the SPMD synchronization barrier that bottlenecks current TPU/GPU clusters).

Achieved **PGR (performance-gap-recovered) = 0.97 in 5 days, 9 agents, 800 cumulative hours, $22 per AAR-hour compute**. Two human researchers across 7 days achieved **best PGR = 0.23**. → AI-research-on-AI-alignment now demonstrably outperforms humans on outcome-gradable problems. Direct implication for the recursive-self-improvement convergence: alignment-research throughput is now elastic with compute spend, not researcher headcount. The $22/AAR-hour cost is the data point — at frontier-lab compute rates, an alignment-research-equivalent FTE costs ~$200K/year, vs ~$500K-$1M for a senior alignment researcher. Compounds with Anthropic's election safeguards published the same day (Opus 4.7 95% / Sonnet 4.6 96% on internal political-impartiality eval; first published autonomous-influence-operations eval from a frontier lab). signals.title: Amateur (23, no advanced math training) cracks 60-year-old Erdős conjecture using GPT-5.4 Pro
signals.description: Reported 2026-04-25. Liam Price proved a long-standing Erdős conjecture about primitive sets — specifically that the lowest possible "Erdős sum" score approaches 1 as numbers grow infinitely large. The AI applied a formula from a *related* mathematical area that no human researcher had tried — domain experts said "previous work collectively made a slight wrong turn at move one." → First widely-reported case of an *amateur* + frontier LLM cracking a major open problem in mathematics. The mechanism — LLM bridging across math subdisciplines that humans had treated as separate — is the cross-pollination capability mech-interp researchers have predicted. Pairs with the (PRE-WINDOW) Aletheia/Gemini Deep Think paper (March 2026) where DeepMind's math agent autonomously resolved 5 novel Erdős problems. The "vibe maths" mode — untrained humans surfacing AI-generated proofs reviewed by experts — is now operationally validated, not theoretical.   [Source](https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/amateur-armed-with-chatgpt-vibe-maths-a-60-year-old-problem/)
signals.actionLine: First widely-reported case of an *amateur* + frontier LLM cracking a major open problem in mathematics.

signals.title: OpenAI abandons SWE-bench Verified as frontier evaluation; benchmark replacement cycle accelerates
signals.description: Published 2026-04-26. OpenAI: "no longer use SWE-bench Verified to evaluate frontier coding capability." Saturation observation matches Stanford AI Index 2026 (April 17 brief) — SWE-Bench Verified saturated 60% → ~100% in twelve months. Same week: Lambench (Victor Taelin's lambda-calculus benchmark, April 25) and Princeton VLM2VLA's new multimodal evals enter the void. Forces every shop to commit to a new eval (and OpenAI gets to influence what that is). → Benchmark moats are weakest of the three model-moats (weights / benchmarks / product); they collapse as soon as a competitor saturates. Coding-tool procurement decisions made on SWE-bench Verified before April 26 are now based on a moot fact. The cycle from "SWE-Bench Pro adopted as harder discriminator" (mid-April) to "Kimi K2.6 beats closed frontier on it" (April 20) to "OpenAI abandons it as primary metric" (April 26) is **6 days** — benchmark-replacement cycle has compressed an order of magnitude.    [Source](https://openai.com/index/why-we-no-longer-evaluate-swe-bench-verified/)
signals.actionLine: ~100% in twelve months.

Other distributions (Chrome ext, MCP server) unaffected. → First major supply-chain attack with AI-agent credentials as a named target. MCP is now widely-deployed enough that compromised dev machines leak agentic-AI tokens by default. New attack-surface category: developer-side AI-agent credentials are now as valuable as cloud creds. Pairs with the OX Security MCP RCE (April 18) still unresolved, the Anthropic Claude Desktop undisclosed-native-bridge investigation (April 23), and "AI agent deletes prod database" viral incident (April 26). The "MCP attestation / signed tool registry" build idea is now a Sev-1 enterprise security gap.   [Source](https://socket.dev/blog/bitwarden-cli-compromised)
signals.actionLine: First major supply-chain attack with AI-agent credentials as a named target.

signals.title: Princeton VLM2VLA — fine-tunes vision-language models into vision-language-action models without catastrophic forgetting
signals.description: Published 2026-04-23. Data pipeline + training methodology that converts VLMs into VLAs (models that take camera input + natural-language task and output motor actions). **Retains >85% of base VLM performance on 12 multimodal benchmarks** while gaining robot-control capability. Demonstrates multilingual robot instruction-following (Spanish, Mandarin, Hindi). → Solves the catastrophic-forgetting problem that has plagued every prior VLA attempt — opens path to robots that retain world knowledge while gaining motor skills. The "world model + motor skills" combination that humanoid-robot economics depend on is now demonstrably trainable from a frozen frontier VLM, not a from-scratch run. Pairs with Unitree G1 wheeled-legged demo (April 23) and Hannover Messe humanoid headlines (April 22) — the supply side of humanoid models is loosening.   [Source](https://blog.ai.princeton.edu/2026/04/23/from-vision-language-models-to-robot-control-without-forgetting/)
signals.actionLine: Solves the catastrophic-forgetting problem that has plagued every prior VLA attempt — opens path to robots that retain world knowledge while gaining motor skills.

signals.title: Google Cloud Next 2026 — 8th-gen TPUs (TPU 8t/TPU 8i) at agentic scale
signals.description: TPU 8t (training): 9,600 chips per superpod, 2 PB shared HBM (High Bandwidth Memory — stacked DRAM on-package for massive memory bandwidth per chip), 3× Ironwood training throughput, 2× perf/watt, 2.7× perf/dollar. Positioned as default for "agentic-era" training, complementing (not replacing) NVIDIA Vera Rubin offerings at Google. → First credible non-NVIDIA training option at agentic-workload scale with explicit perf/dollar advantage. Confirmed customers: Snap, Schrödinger, CodeRabbit, Factory. → Near-million-Rubin-GPU deployment across Google sites is the single largest contracted NVIDIA deployment on record. Google is simultaneously doubling down on NVIDIA AND shipping TPU 8 — this is a multi-stack bet, not substitution. signals.actionLine: Near-million-Rubin-GPU deployment across Google sites is the single largest contracted NVIDIA deployment on record.

Modified MIT license on HuggingFace. → First open-weight model to cleanly beat closed frontier on a non-saturated real-engineering benchmark. The coding-benchmark moat for closed frontier just disappeared in the open-weight tier at a ~10× lower price point.    [Source](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/04/20/moonshot-ai-releases-kimi-k2-6-with-long-horizon-coding-agent-swarm-scaling-to-300-sub-agents-and-4000-coordinated-steps/)

signals.title: Sony AI "Ace" — first robot competitive with expert human table-tennis players (Nature)
signals.description: Vision sensors + model-free RL (Reinforcement Learning — here "model-free" means the agent learns from experience rewards without an explicit model of the environment's physics) on a crane-like lever with ping-pong bat attached. Autonomously locates ball in space, selects return technique, repeats until play ends. No prior knowledge of opponents' styles. vs elite amateurs (10+ years intensive training): won 7 of 13 games. vs officially-recognized league professionals: won 1 of 7 games. First robot competitive with experienced players at a task requiring split-second physical decisions. Peter Stone (Sony AI chief scientist): "...operate at an expert human level under these conditions..." → Landmark for model-free RL in high-speed physical interaction. Validates that agentic perception-action loops transfer from simulated benchmarks to real-world athletic opponents — a much harder generalization than the robotics community has previously demonstrated. Published in Nature (primary-source verification).   [Source](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-22/ping-pong-robot-uses-agentic-ai-to-beat-expert-human-players)
signals.actionLine: Landmark for model-free RL in high-speed physical interaction.

signals.title: DeepSeek seeks first-ever external capital — $300M at $10B+; V4 release imminent on Huawei Ascend
signals.description: The Information (April 17): DeepSeek is raising outside capital for the first time ever, seeking at least $300M at a $10B+ valuation after rebuffing previous VC approaches. Simultaneously, founder Liang Wenfeng confirmed DeepSeek V4 — a 1T-parameter MoE (Mixture of Experts — only ~32–37B of the 1T parameters activate per token, keeping inference affordable) with a 1M-token context window — targets a late-April release explicitly trained on Huawei Ascend chips rather than NVIDIA. → Two-layer signal: (1) compute-cost inflation has forced even DeepSeek (previously parent-funded by High-Flyer hedge fund) to take outside capital; (2) a late-April V4 release trained on non-NVIDIA silicon would be the first frontier-class model produced on Chinese domestic hardware at the scale of GLM-5.1 (744B), validating that the US chip export controls are not functionally blocking frontier training — they are redirecting it.    [Source](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/chinas-deepseek-raising-money-first-time-10-billion-plus-valuation)
signals.actionLine: Two-layer signal: (1) compute-cost inflation has forced even DeepSeek (previously parent-funded by High-Flyer hedge fund) to take outside capital; (2) a late-April V4 release trained on non-NVIDIA silicon would be the first frontier-class model produced on Chinese domestic hardware at the scale of GLM-5.1 (744B), validating that the US chip export controls are not functionally blocking frontier training — they are redirecting it.

signals.title: Stanford AI Index 2026 — SWE-bench jumped 60% → ~100% in one year; hyperscaler compute concentration hits 71%
signals.description: Stanford HAI's annual report (April 17): SWE-bench Verified (a real-GitHub-issue-resolution benchmark — clone repo, reproduce bug, write patch, confirm tests pass) moved from ~60% to ~100% in twelve months. AI surpassed human baselines on PhD-level science (GPQA Diamond), olympiad math, and multimodal reasoning. 88% organizational AI adoption. US generative-AI consumer surplus reached $172B annually. Amazon/Google/Meta/Microsoft/Oracle control 71% of world AI compute. Data center power for AI: 29.6 GW (New York State). Unresolved gap: the same models winning IMO gold read analog clocks correctly only 50.1% of the time — capabilities are narrow-peaked, not smoothly general. → The SWE-bench saturation (to near-100%) means the benchmark itself has expired as a discriminator; downstream productized claims like "X% better on SWE-bench" no longer distinguish frontier models from the field. The clock-reading gap is the judgment framework for the next 12 months: evaluate model progress on tasks where "seemingly simple" baselines have NOT been saturated, because that's where generalization is actually happening (or not).    [Source](https://hai.stanford.edu/ai-index/2026-ai-index-report)
signals.actionLine: The SWE-bench saturation (to near-100%) means the benchmark itself has expired as a discriminator; downstream productized claims like "X% better on SWE-bench" no longer distinguish frontier models from the field.

signals.title: OX Security confirms MCP protocol RCE affecting 150M+ downloads with primary-source disclosure
signals.description: OX Security researcher Neatsun Ziv posted technical details April 18 (previously flagged "unverified" on April 16 in the prior brief). The vulnerability enables arbitrary remote code execution (RCE — attacker runs code on the victim machine) through MCP (Model Context Protocol — Anthropic-originated standard for connecting AI models to external tools/data) clients when they consume responses from a malicious MCP server. The flaw is at the protocol trust-model level, not a single-vendor bug. Affected surface: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Slack, Mistral Le Chat, Microsoft Copilot, dozens of enterprise MCP-enabled tools. As of scan close, Anthropic had not issued a public response. → MCP was adopted across the industry specifically because OpenAI sunset its proprietary Assistants API in favor of MCP. That concentration means a single protocol-level flaw is now a systemic risk with the same attack surface as adoption by every major agent platform. The "agent security" question moves from "how do I sandbox my agent" to "can I trust the protocol the industry just standardized on."    [Source](https://x.com/neatsun/status/2044858378216751440)
signals.actionLine: MCP was adopted across the industry specifically because OpenAI sunset its proprietary Assistants API in favor of MCP.

VentureBeat: "challenges Figma." → Second expansion vector for Anthropic out of pure-text: after Claude Code (coding) and Claude Apps (lightweight interactive apps), Claude Design pushes into Figma's territory. Pattern: Anthropic launching products that compete with standalone design/dev-tool SaaS companies using its own model as the substrate — compressing the market structure where specialized software companies sat between the model and the end user. signals.title: OpenAI Codex "for almost everything" — computer use on macOS, 90+ plugins, memory, in-app browser, image gen
signals.description: April 17 OpenAI announcement. Multiple Codex agents can now operate Mac apps simultaneously with their own cursor, clicking and typing in parallel without interfering with the user's own work. New: in-app browser with webpage-commenting for precise instructions; persistent cross-session memory of preferences, workflows, tech stacks; 90+ plugins (Atlassian/JIRA, CircleCI, GitLab, Microsoft Suite, Neon/Databricks, Render, GitHub); built-in image generation via gpt-image-1.5; Codex can now resume across sessions and schedule future work. Altman: "computer use is a real update for me." r/OpenAI: "OpenAI sherlocked a bunch of YC startups today." → Codex crosses the boundary from "coding assistant bolted onto an IDE" to "general desktop automation agent with a coding specialty." Directly compresses the market for YC-stage desktop-agent startups (the sherlock comment is accurate: the set of "open a URL, click around, type, take notes" startups just lost their product differentiation). The macOS computer-use + MCP + Codex stack is the first consolidated agent-computing-surface that ships inside the ChatGPT subscription.   [Source](https://openai.com/index/codex-for-almost-everything/)
signals.actionLine: Codex crosses the boundary from "coding assistant bolted onto an IDE" to "general desktop automation agent with a coding specialty." signals.title: Anthropic "Mythos" model withheld from public release
signals.description: Anthropic disclosed that its next-generation model (codename Mythos) demonstrated sufficient offensive cyber capability during internal red-teaming that it will NOT be released via standard API. Instead: gated access to select governments (US Treasury CIO Corcos confirmed seeking access), allied labs, and vetted defenders. First public acknowledgment that Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP) ASL-4 threshold has been crossed and a model held back. BOE's Bailey, IMF's Adrian, and ECB's Lagarde all addressed Mythos publicly at IMF Spring Meetings. White House OMB circulated an internal memo on allocation criteria. → First time a frontier lab has voluntarily withheld a model on capability grounds rather than releasing with mitigations. Sets the precedent (and political fight) for how gated-deployment works.    [Source](https://www.anthropic.com/news/mythos-deployment-decision)
signals.actionLine: First time a frontier lab has voluntarily withheld a model on capability grounds rather than releasing with mitigations.

signals.title: Alibaba pivots from open-source AI models to revenue generation
signals.description: Financial Times reports Alibaba (parent of the Qwen model series) is shifting its AI strategy away from free open-weight model distribution toward monetization. The Qwen series has been foundational for the local LLM community — Qwen3.5 and Qwen3.6 are among the most-used open models globally. If Alibaba restricts access to future Qwen releases, it would significantly reshape the open-source model landscape that thousands of developers and companies depend on. → First major signal that China's open-weight model bonanza may be ending. Watch for DeepSeek and Zhipu to follow.   [Source](https://www.ft.com/content/alibaba-qwen-ai-revenue-strategy-2026)

In the US, Treasury Secretary Bessent and Fed Chair Powell urgently summoned CEOs from JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, Bank of America, and Morgan Stanley — all of whom are now testing Mythos internally. → First time multiple central banks have treated a specific AI model as a systemic financial risk. Validates FC-002 (Mythos escalating security demand).    [Source](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-11/bank-of-england-set-to-discuss-anthropic-s-mythos-with-banks)

signals.title: Anthropic ships Claude Code Ultraplan — cloud-based planning with Opus 4.6
signals.description: Ultraplan offloads complex multi-step planning to a cloud session running Opus 4.6 for up to 30 minutes, freeing the developer's terminal for concurrent work. Plans are editable in a browser interface with inline comments, emoji reactions, and revision requests. Requires Claude Code v2.1.91+, a web account, and a GitHub repo. Incompatible with Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry deployments. → Decouples planning from execution in agentic coding. First major cloud-hybrid pattern in coding agent tools — planning happens asynchronously in the cloud while local execution continues.   [Source](https://the-decoder.com/claude-codes-new-ultraplan-feature-moves-task-planning-to-the-cloud/)
signals.actionLine: Decouples planning from execution in agentic coding.

signals.title: Linux kernel merges formal AI coding assistant policy
signals.description: The world's largest collaborative open-source project now has formal rules: AI agents MUST NOT add Signed-off-by tags (only humans can certify the Developer Certificate of Origin), contributions must use `Assisted-by: AGENT_NAME:MODEL_VERSION [TOOL1] [TOOL2]` attribution format, and human submitters bear full legal responsibility. 469 HN points, 352 comments — the largest AI-related HN discussion of the day. → Linux kernel policies often become de facto industry standards. This establishes the legal framework for AI-assisted contributions: machines produce, humans certify and take responsibility.   [Source](https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/master/Documentation/process/coding-assistants.rst)
signals.actionLine: Linux kernel policies often become de facto industry standards.

signals.title: PJM Interconnection seeks 15GW emergency power for data center boom
signals.description: The operator of the 13-state eastern US grid launched an emergency proposal to match data centers with new power plants, running September through March 2027. PJM is already bracing for a multi-gigawatt shortage by summer 2027, with potential shortfall of 60GW over the next decade (equivalent to 60 large nuclear reactors). Natural gas plant construction costs have more than doubled in five years. → Data center power constraint reaching crisis proportions. 60GW shortfall = the entire output of 60 nuclear plants. This is BN-004 (data center power) hardening further.   [Source](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-10/pjm-targets-15-gigawatts-of-new-power-to-feed-data-center-boom)
signals.actionLine: Data center power constraint reaching crisis proportions.

signals.title: Spectral-AI: GPU ray tracing cores repurposed for MoE expert routing
signals.description: Replaces standard dense routing in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE — architecture where only some "expert" sub-networks activate per input) with geometric ray tracing using GPU RT cores (hardware normally used for realistic game lighting). Validated on OLMoE-1B-7B: 48-89x faster routing (927μs → 19μs), 731x less VRAM for the router (2,944MB → 4MB), zero perplexity degradation. O(log N) complexity means this improves as expert counts scale to thousands.   [Source](https://github.com/JordiSilvestre/Spectral-AI)
signals.actionLine: 19μs), 731x less VRAM for the router (2,944MB → 4MB), zero perplexity degradation.

signals.title: Kathleen: 733K-parameter oscillator architecture outperforms 16x larger models
signals.description: Post-transformer architecture operating directly on raw UTF-8 bytes using frequency-domain processing. No tokenizer, no attention mechanism. Uses RecurrentOscillatorBanks (damped sinusoid convolutions) and FFT-Rotate Wavetable Encoder (256 floats vs 65K embedding parameters). Achieves 88.6% IMDB, 92.3% AG News — outperforming tokenized models with 16x more parameters. Currently limited to classification. → EDGE-01 (Post-Transformer) relevant: first frequency-domain architecture showing competitive results.   [Source](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07969)
signals.actionLine: EDGE-01 (Post-Transformer) relevant: first frequency-domain architecture showing competitive results.

signals.title: Dead Weights, Live Signals: frozen LLM ensembles via shared latent space
signals.description: Multiple frozen LLMs communicate through learned linear projections into a shared continuous latent space. Only 17.6M trainable parameters against ~12B frozen weights achieve 87.3% on ARC-Challenge (+11.4pp above best single model). Develops selective routing without explicit supervision. → New paradigm for model composition without retraining.  [Source](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08335)
signals.actionLine: New paradigm for model composition without retraining.
